name: Heroes of Science and Fiction
short_description: A turn-based sci-fi strategy that combines exploration and exciting clashes on the battlefield. Take control over several different species, raise powerful armies for your heroes and destroy your opponents! This HoMM-like is set in a scifi universe, with new mechanics on top of the classic formula.
detailed_description:
Heroes of Science and Fiction (formerly known as Silence of the Siren) is a turn-based strategy game where several different space-faring civilizations fight over control of a distant star system. Explore what’s left on the planets and secure resources to build mighty cities. Raise powerful armies and defeat your opponents on the battlefield!

Content in the full game

Heroes of Science and Fiction contains 5 playable factions, four campaigns with 4 levels each and 30+ skirmish maps set in colorful environments.

Each faction brings its own base, 7 upgradeable unit types, a special skill and a set of commanders to choose from. There are 24 skills, 54 active commander abilities and various pieces of equipment, consumables and artifacts.

The Siren star system, birthplace of a galactic alliance, and the homeworld of a lost Primals civilization, has been cut from the rest of the galaxy. As a result, the carefully built alliance split into bickering factions and secluded colonies. These days, hardly anyone pays attention to the ruins scattered all over the few habitable planets of a freshly isolated system. The deeper you venture into the uncharted regions, the stronger the feeling of endangerment grows. There is something else. An entity, a shade, an echo of whispers from below.

Explore what's left on the planets and secure important resources. Use them to upgrade your bases with advanced technology and to hire massive armies. Play as several different factions, recruit skilled commanders, teach them new skills and lead them into battle. Overcome obstacles on the map to gather powerful equipment and ancient artifacts.

Encounter a wide range of enemies in turn-based combat, use your tactical thinking to gain advantage on the battlefield. Learn the unique moves of your units, deploy your commander's special abilities and outsmart even much stronger opponents.

  • Explore a rich sci-fi world and discover secrets of a lost civilization

  • Collect powerful artifacts, secure important resources, upgrade your bases and hire the strongest units

  • Level-up your commanders and crush your opponents in exciting turn-based battles

  • Take control over several different species and factions

  • Old-school strategy game with modern controls and intuitive UI

supported_languages: English, French, German, Spanish - Spain, Czech, Polish, Simplified Chinese, Hungarian, Portuguese - Brazil, Russian

reviews.review_text:
Only based on campaign because that's all I've played so far but balance is totally whack. If you actually manage heroes' movement like fans of the genre would and clear the first part of the campaign ASAP, second part of the map opens up and AI doomstack appears.

Solution? Without advancing a plot you just end turns so you get more reinforcements from base. And about map buildings, bio hatchery outright increase stack you leave inside by 50 percent. I end up using it to fight that aforementioned doomstack and this does not feel right at all. Either I just end turns doing nothing for like 16 phases or use that overpowered map building, just because I was very fast at clearing the first part of the map.

I feel dumb playing campaign and won't continue. Can't recommend.

Classic turn-based strategy, exactly what the trailer promises. Based on a beloved fantasy classic but in a fresh sci-fi setting. The gameplay is smooth and challenging, while the script doesn't take itself too seriously. 
Very polished, excellent UI, beautiful graphics, a subtle day/night system that matters for some decisions, and a fantastic soundtrack. 
Highly enjoyable. Strongly recommended.

It's OK. I beat 3/4 missions of the first campaign and I think I can talk about it now. It's a modern complete overhaul of HoMM 3, with some fresh ideas. A bit slow to load and has some performance dips on my machine. Loading in/out of the city an combat also causes the game to hiccup for a second. Together it adds up to the game being a bit slow to play, even with maxed out animation speed. Missing some QOL features like quickly splitting units and auto-resolving easy battles. But the gameplay is fine. Pick this one up if you like the genre.

I have close to 50  hours already since I got it during the early access and I love it so far.
I like the feel of the heroes of might and magic vibes.
What I dislike is the lack of special skills for heroes, they should have extra bonuses
for their starting skill so that you can have your own favorite special hero like
Heroes 3 does.

One thing that I ran into is a soft lock on the last mission of the third campain. 
Once you cross the bridge "Tin" is in front of "Professor Zane". When I try to select Tin
I can no longer do anything.
This means that both of these commanders as stuch in their position at the bridge
since Tin is in front of Zane. Will play some more to see if I can finish the mission
while I can't move them. Hopefully this can be fixed as a patch post launch.
EDIT:
Was able to unstuck Tin by giving him a teleport consumable found next to a base.
Once he got to base I was able to select him without the game freezing.
Glad I could get over this with this in game workaround, hopefully it can be fixed
to avoid needing a workaround in the first place.

The general writing, grammar and spelling are sloppy and it just puts me off hugely. Happening is spelled wrong when there's a new week, things that you see constantly all the time which seem pretty minor to fix shouldn't be so badly written.

The description of the first general in the soviet mole army is just complete nonsense and while I think I get what they WANT to say it's just not working for me at all. 

The abilities are strangely named and I don't feel inspired by them. With it being inspired by HOMM (and I assume HOMM3 specifically) I expected some character and feeling to the abilities and skills I pick up but it just doesn't have it. Field Research for example gives me random abilities every week. Why? It just doesn't feel right at all and all the skills have this feel to them. You also tend to get a new skill every level and there's not many so it makes the heroes feel very generic - which has been mentioned in a ton of reviews.

I do like the variety in the cities to build and some of the concepts but I feel like the map looks hugely busy and the size of the combat map makes ranged units so much stronger than they should be. They just need to have a balance pass and then get someone to re-write the campaign for them.
